title: Sunrun Q2 2023 Earnings Summary
text: Sunrun exceeded expectations with Q2 2023 quarterly earnings of $0.25 per share, surpassing consensus estimate of $0.34 loss—a 173.53% earnings surprise. Company showed improvement compared to year-ago period's $0.06 loss per share. The solar energy company generated $590.19 million in quarterly revenue, falling short of analyst expectations by 5.09%, representing slight increase from prior year's $584.58 million. Over past four quarters, Sunrun exceeded consensus earnings estimates three times and topped revenue expectations same number of times. Shares declined approximately 20.3% since start of 2023, underperforming S&P 500's 19.2% gain during same timeframe. Sunrun installed 296.6 Megawatts of Solar Energy Capacity in Q2, significantly exceeding high-end of guidance, reaching 6.2 Gigawatts of Networked Solar Capacity. Customer additions reached 39,755 in Q2 with 869,464 total customers as of June 30, 2023. Annual Recurring Revenue from Subscribers exceeded $1.1 billion as of June 30, 2023. Company carried Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating, suggesting shares should perform in line with market in near future. Management's upcoming earnings call commentary was critical for determining stock's immediate price direction.
